qa_pairs.question: How do I include an interpreted User-Defined Function (UDF) in a Fluent simulation using a journal file?
qa_pairs.answer: To include an interpreted UDF, add the command `define/user-defined/interpreted-functions "sampleudf.c" "cpp" 10000 no` to your journal file before the cas/dat files are read or initialized. Replace "sampleudf.c" with the name of your UDF source file.

qa_pairs.question: What is the command to specify an interpreted UDF in a Fluent journal file?
qa_pairs.answer: The command to specify an interpreted UDF in a Fluent journal file is `define/user-defined/interpreted-functions "sampleudf.c" "cpp" 10000 no`.

qa_pairs.question: Is the command for an interpreted UDF different for serial and parallel Fluent simulations?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the command for an interpreted UDF remains the same regardless of whether the simulation is run in serial or parallel.

qa_pairs.question: What steps should be taken to ensure an interpreted UDF is found in the same directory as the journal file?
qa_pairs.answer: To ensure the UDF is found, open your cas file in the Fluent GUI, remove any managed definitions, and then resave it. This will ensure that only the journal file commands control the UDF when Fluent runs.

qa_pairs.question: What is required for an interpreted UDF to work with parallel Fluent jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: For an interpreted UDF to be used with parallel Fluent jobs, it will need to be parallelized.
